info.name: mHealth API
info.description:
Complete API collection for the mHealth MassHealth benefits platform.

**Setup:**
1. Set `baseUrl` variable to your running server (default: `http://localhost:3000`)
2. Set `authToken` to a valid Supabase JWT (`Authorization: Bearer <token>`)
3. For dev-only auth endpoints, no token is needed.

**Dev auth quick-start:**
- POST `/api/auth/dev-register` → copy the returned token → paste into the `authToken` collection variable

variable.description: Supabase JWT — paste the token returned by POST /api/auth/dev-register
UUID of an existing application draft

request.description: Verifies the database connection is live. Returns `{ ok: true }` on success.

item.name: Auth (Dev Only)
item.description: Development-only helpers for creating and confirming test accounts. Not available in production.

request.description: Creates a new Supabase user and returns a JWT token. Copy the `token` into the `authToken` collection variable.
Bypasses email confirmation for a dev user. Call after `dev-register` if email confirmation is enabled in Supabase.

item.name: Applications
item.description: Application draft lifecycle: create, list, fetch, and update wizard state.

query.description: Filter by status (draft, submitted, approved, denied)

request.description: Returns a paginated list of application drafts for the authenticated user.
Creates a new application draft. Provide a client-generated UUID for `applicationId`.
Fetches the wizard state for a specific application draft.
Saves the current wizard state (step + formData). Call on each step transition.

item.name: Address
item.description: Address validation via Nominatim (OSM) or Google Geocoding API.

request.description: Geocodes and validates a US address. Returns `valid: true/false` plus a normalized suggestion and coordinates.

response.name: 200 Valid
200 Invalid

item.name: Chat — MassHealth
item.description:
Conversational AI powered by Ollama (llama3.2) with three operating modes:

| Mode | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `assistant` | General MassHealth Q&A with RAG policy context |
| `application_intake` | Guided conversational application intake |
| `benefit_advisor` | **Extract structured eligibility facts + run rule engine + explain results** |

All requests require `Authorization: Bearer {{authToken}}`.

request.description:
General MassHealth policy Q&A. Uses RAG to retrieve relevant policy chunks before answering. Rejects off-topic messages with `outOfScope: true`.

**Supported languages:** `en`, `es`, `pt`, `zh`, `vi`, `ht`

response.name: 200 On-topic
200 Out of scope

request.description:
Conversational intake that guides the user through a MassHealth application question by question. Extracts household relationship hints from user messages to avoid repeating questions.

**applicationType** (optional): `MassHealth`, `SNAP`, `ConnectorCare`, etc.

response.name: 200 Next question

request.description:
**Structured eligibility extraction + rule engine evaluation.**

Uses `mode: "benefit_advisor"` which runs three steps:
1. LLM extracts structured eligibility facts (`factsExtracted`) from the conversation
2. If sufficient facts are present, the rule engine runs and produces program-level eligibility results
3. LLM generates a natural-language explanation grounded by RAG policy context

**Extracted fact fields:**
- `livesInMA` — boolean
- `age` — integer years
- `householdSize` — integer
- `annualIncome` — USD (converts monthly × 12 automatically)
- `isPregnant` — boolean
- `hasDisability` — boolean (true if applicant receives SSI/SSDI)
- `hasMedicare` — boolean
- `hasEmployerInsurance` — boolean
- `citizenshipStatus` — `citizen` | `qualified_immigrant` | `undocumented` | `other`

**Minimum facts for rule engine:** `age`, `householdSize`, `annualIncome`.
If minimum facts are absent, `eligibilityResults` is `null` and the LLM asks follow-up questions.

**Use-case:** Call this at the end of a benefit_advisor conversation to get a machine-readable eligibility summary alongside the human-readable reply.

response.name: 200 — Facts extracted + rule engine ran
response.body:
{
  "ok": true,
  "outOfScope": false,
  "reply": "Based on what you've shared, it looks like you may qualify for MassHealth CarePlus. Your household of 3 with $42,000 annual income is at approximately 155% FPL, which falls within the CarePlus limit of 133% FPL for adults...",
  "factsExtracted": {
    "livesInMA": true,
    "age": 34,
    "householdSize": 3,
    "annualIncome": 42000,
    "citizenshipStatus": "citizen",
    "hasEmployerInsurance": false
  },
  "eligibilityResults": {
    "fplPercent": 155,
    "annualFPL": 27120,
    "summary": "Income is 155% FPL — likely eligible for MassHealth CarePlus",
    "results": [
      {
        "program": "MassHealth CarePlus",
        "status": "likely_eligible",
        "tagline": "Full coverage for adults 21–64 up to 133% FPL",
        "actionLabel": "Apply Now",
        "actionHref": "/apply/masshealth",
        "color": "green"
      },
      {
        "program": "ConnectorCare",
        "status": "likely_eligible",
        "tagline": "Subsidized coverage 100–300% FPL",
        "actionLabel": "View Plans",
        "actionHref": "/apply/connector-care",
        "color": "green"
      },
      {
        "program": "SNAP",
        "status": "review_needed",
        "tagline": "Food assistance — income and asset review required",
        "actionLabel": "Check Eligibility",
        "actionHref": "/prescreener",
        "color": "yellow"
      }
    ]
  }
}

response.name: 200 — Insufficient facts (asks follow-up)
response.body:
{
  "ok": true,
  "outOfScope": false,
  "reply": "To check your eligibility I'll need a few details. How old are you, and how many people are in your household?",
  "factsExtracted": {},
  "eligibilityResults": null
}

request.description:
Multi-turn benefit advisor conversation. Pass the full message history on each turn — the server is stateless. The `factsExtracted` field accumulates across turns as the user provides more information.

Same `mode: "benefit_advisor"` as **Extract Structured Eligibility Facts**, shown here with a realistic multi-turn history.

response.name: 200 Multi-turn result

item.name: Benefit Orchestration
item.description: Multi-program benefit stack evaluation. Evaluates a `FamilyProfile` against 9 MA benefit programs simultaneously using a rule engine (no LLM).

request.description: Retrieves the current user's saved family profile. Returns `null` if no profile exists yet.

body.raw:
{
  "age": 34,
  "householdSize": 3,
  "annualIncome": 42000,
  "citizenshipStatus": "citizen",
  "isPregnant": false,
  "hasDisability": false,
  "hasMedicare": false,
  "hasEmployerInsurance": false,
  "householdMembers": [
    { "age": 32, "relationship": "spouse", "isPregnant": false, "hasDisability": false },
    { "age": 7, "relationship": "child", "isPregnant": false, "hasDisability": false }
  ]
}

request.description:
Creates or updates the family profile for the authenticated user (upsert). Does not run the rule engine — use `/evaluate` for that.

**citizenshipStatus:** `citizen` | `qualified_immigrant` | `undocumented` | `other`

body.raw:
{
  "age": 34,
  "householdSize": 3,
  "annualIncome": 42000,
  "citizenshipStatus": "citizen",
  "isPregnant": false,
  "hasDisability": false,
  "hasMedicare": false,
  "hasEmployerInsurance": false,
  "householdMembers": [
    { "age": 32, "relationship": "spouse", "isPregnant": false, "hasDisability": false },
    { "age": 7, "relationship": "child", "isPregnant": false, "hasDisability": false }
  ]
}

request.description:
Saves the family profile and immediately runs the benefit stack rule engine across 9 programs: MassHealth, MSP, SNAP, EITC, Section 8, Child Care, LIHEAP, WIC, TAFDC/EAEDC.

Returns per-program eligibility with status, estimated value, and action links. Persists the result for audit history.

response.name: 200 Stack evaluated

request.description:
Uploads a PDF and extracts its content as structured JSON. Uses `multipart/form-data` with field name `file`.

**Limits:** 10 MB max. PDF files only (by MIME type or `.pdf` extension).
Generates a filled PDF from a named template and field data. Returns a binary PDF stream (`application/pdf`).

item.name: Forms
item.description: Pre-built form filling for specific MA benefit forms.

request.description:
Fills and returns a completed ACA-3 (Rev. 03/2025) MassHealth application form as a PDF. Accepts a flat JSON payload matching the ACA-3 field schema.

See `samples/aca-3-0325-payload.sample.json` for the full field reference.

item.name: RAG — Document Ingestion
item.description: Staff-only endpoint for ingesting policy documents into the pgvector RAG store. Requires an ingest secret key in addition to authentication.

request.description:
Fetches, chunks, and embeds all configured `POLICY_SOURCES` into the pgvector store. This is a long-running operation (up to 5 minutes). Run after deploying new policy documents.

**Requires:** `X-Ingest-Key` header matching the `RAG_INGEST_SECRET` environment variable.
Ingests a single custom document by URL into the RAG store. Useful for one-off policy additions without re-ingesting all sources.
